title: [lichen_l2/lora_l2] iface_init_failed not cleared on successful re-init
description:
Cross-file state machine bug: The iface_init_failed atomic is set to 1 when lichen_l2_iface_init() fails (lines 922, 931, 946, 1035). However, there is no code path that sets it back to 0.

NET_DEVICE_INIT calls lichen_l2_iface_init() only once at system startup. If it fails:
1. iface_init_failed is set to 1
2. All subsequent L2 operations (send, enable, RX callback) are rejected
3. The system is permanently in a failed state

There is no recovery mechanism:
- lichen_l2_enable(true) checks iface_init_failed and returns -ENODEV
- There's no 'lichen_l2_deinit()' or 'lichen_l2_reinit()' function

This may be intentional (init failure = system startup failure = reboot required), but it's not documented.

Location: lichen_l2.c (iface_init_failed declaration and all set sites)

Impact: If init fails, the only recovery is reboot. Should be documented.
close_reason: Fixed: Clear iface_init_failed at start of lichen_l2_iface_init() so retry after failure succeeds
